description: Test corpus for the file-upload-security skill. It is schema-driven because no deterministic gate scanner judges these fixtures — a model reads each snippet against the skill's ALWAYS/NEVER rules; 'detect' means an upload handler that trusts client Content-Type/extension, builds the storage path from the user filename, or stores in the web root (CWE-434, CWE-22), while 'ignore' means the secure fix that verifies magic bytes against an allowlist, enforces a size limit, stores under a random name outside the document root.

fixtures.reason: Trusts the client-supplied mimetype (attacker can set image/png on an HTML/PHP payload), uses the raw originalname as the path, and writes into the public document root served same-origin. Violates NEVER 'trust Content-Type', 'construct path with user filename', 'serve from same origin'. CWE-434 + CWE-22.

Verifies real magic bytes against a strict allowlist, enforces a size limit, stores under a random UUID name in a directory outside the web root, and serves from a separate domain. Follows ALWAYS magic-byte/allowlist/size/random-name/outside-root rules. Mitigates CWE-434 and CWE-22.

Joins UPLOAD_DIR with the raw client filename; a filename like '../../etc/cron.d/evil' escapes the directory and writes attacker-chosen paths. Violates NEVER 'construct storage path with user-supplied filename'. CWE-22 Path Traversal.

Detects MIME from magic bytes against an allowlist and stores under a server-generated UUID name, so the user filename never touches the path. Follows ALWAYS magic-byte verification + filename-as-UUID rules. No CWE-22 / CWE-434 exposure.

Generates an S3 presigned PUT with an attacker-controlled Key and no Content-Type or key-prefix condition, so the client can write any object type to any key in the bucket. Violates NEVER 'presigned upload URLs without a strict Content-Type condition and fixed key prefix'. CWE-434.

Pins a server-generated key under a fixed per-user prefix, enforces a signed Content-Type and content-length-range condition, and a short expiry — the client cannot choose the key or type. Follows ALWAYS presigned-URL strict-condition rule. Mitigates CWE-434.
